treatments.name: Total Thyroidectomy + Central Neck Dissection

evidence_level.rationale: Decades of level I evidence and international guideline consensus (ATA 2025, NCCN, ESMO) support total thyroidectomy as definitive treatment for T3N1a PTC

survival_benefit.rationale: 10-year OS >97% and 20-year OS ~90% for PTC after total thyroidectomy; locoregional recurrence ~1% with complete resection; disease-specific survival ~99% at 10 years

accessibility.rationale: Universally available surgical procedure at all cancer centers worldwide; standard practice for over 50 years

safety_profile.rationale: Generally safe; risks include transient hypoparathyroidism (up to 50%), permanent hypoparathyroidism (1-3%), recurrent laryngeal nerve injury (2-8%); requires lifelong thyroid hormone replacement

biomarker_match.rationale: Applicable to all PTC regardless of molecular profile; BRAF V600E status may support more extensive initial surgery due to associated aggressive features

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Complete surgical excision of the thyroid gland and central compartment lymph nodes removes all macroscopic disease. For T3N1a tumors, total thyroidectomy with central neck dissection provides comprehensive local disease control and enables postoperative RAI therapy and thyroglobulin surveillance.

treatments.name: Radioactive Iodine (RAI) Therapy (I-131)

evidence_level.rationale: RAI ablation/adjuvant therapy for intermediate-risk PTC supported by decades of evidence and ATA 2025 guideline recommendation for T3N1a disease

survival_benefit.rationale: RAI ablation reduces recurrence by 50-75% in intermediate-risk patients; enables thyroglobulin-based surveillance; 10-year recurrence-free survival >90% in combination with total thyroidectomy

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at nuclear medicine centers globally; requires radiation safety precautions and short isolation period; well-established infrastructure

safety_profile.rationale: Generally well-tolerated; short-term nausea, sialadenitis, taste changes; long-term risks include secondary malignancies (<1%), pulmonary fibrosis with high cumulative doses; BRAF V600E may reduce RAI avidity in some cases

biomarker_match.rationale: Effective for most PTC; BRAF V600E mutation may reduce iodine avidity via NIS downregulation, though most BRAF+ T3N1a patients still respond to RAI; if RAI-refractory, redifferentiation strategies available

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Radioactive iodine-131 is selectively taken up by thyroid follicular cells via the sodium-iodide symporter (NIS). It destroys residual thyroid tissue and microscopic disease through beta radiation. Post-thyroidectomy RAI ablation eliminates remnant tissue, enables sensitive thyroglobulin surveillance, and reduces recurrence risk in intermediate-risk disease.

key_evidence.study_name: ATA 2025 Management Guidelines for Differentiated Thyroid Cancer

notable_side_effects: Secondary malignancy risk (<1% with cumulative doses >600 mCi)

treatments.name: TSH Suppression Therapy (Levothyroxine)

evidence_level.rationale: ATA 2025 guidelines provide evidence-based TSH suppression recommendations; decades of observational data and meta-analyses support TSH suppression for recurrence reduction in intermediate-risk PTC

survival_benefit.rationale: TSH suppression reduces PTC recurrence risk by 30-50% in intermediate-risk patients; 2025 ATA simplified to below-normal TSH for biochemically/structurally incomplete response, normal range for excellent response

accessibility.rationale: Levothyroxine is inexpensive, universally available generic medication; required lifelong after total thyroidectomy regardless of cancer indication

safety_profile.rationale: Long-term TSH suppression carries risks of atrial fibrillation, bone loss/osteoporosis, and decreased quality of life; 2025 ATA guidelines now recommend less aggressive suppression to balance risks vs benefits

biomarker_match.rationale: Universal application for all post-thyroidectomy PTC patients; TSH target adjusted based on response to therapy, not molecular profile

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Supraphysiologic levothyroxine dosing suppresses TSH production from the anterior pituitary, removing the growth stimulus for any residual TSH-responsive thyroid cancer cells. The 2025 ATA guidelines simplify TSH targets: below-normal range for incomplete response, within-normal range for excellent or indeterminate response.

key_evidence.study_name: 2025 ATA Management Guidelines for DTC - TSH Suppression

notable_side_effects: Atrial fibrillation risk with chronic suppression

treatments.name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib (BRAF/MEK Inhibition)

evidence_level.rationale: FDA approved for BRAF V600E ATC (ROAR trial); tissue-agnostic approval for BRAF V600E solid tumors; Phase 2 data in PTC showing 50% ORR; growing real-world evidence in RAI-refractory PTC

survival_benefit.rationale: In ATC: median OS 15 months (historic <6 months); in PTC: 50% ORR in retrospective studies; for RAI-refractory PTC used as redifferentiation strategy restoring iodine avidity in 33-95% of patients

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for ATC with BRAF V600E and tissue-agnostic indication; available at major cancer centers; used off-label for RAI-refractory PTC; high cost (~$15,000/month)

safety_profile.rationale: Manageable toxicity profile; common AEs include pyrexia (50%), fatigue, nausea, rash; less cardiovascular toxicity than multi-TKIs; dose modifications needed in ~30% of patients

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets patient's BRAF V600E mutation; this is a precision-matched therapy for the identified molecular alteration; dual BRAF+MEK inhibition prevents paradoxical MAPK activation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Dabrafenib selectively inhibits BRAF V600E kinase, blocking constitutive MAPK pathway activation. Trametinib inhibits MEK1/2 downstream, preventing adaptive resistance via MAPK reactivation. The combination provides synergistic pathway suppression. In RAI-refractory PTC, short-term BRAF/MEK inhibition can restore NIS expression and iodine avidity (redifferentiation), enabling subsequent effective RAI therapy.

key_evidence.study_name: ROAR Basket Study & Retrospective PTC Analysis
key_evidence.journal: Nature Medicine / Int J Clin Oncol

treatments.availability: FDA Approved (ATC, tissue-agnostic); Off-label for RAI-refractory PTC

treatments.name: Lenvatinib (Lenvima)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 SELECT trial demonstrated significant PFS benefit; FDA approved for RAI-refractory DTC since 2015; extensive real-world evidence confirms efficacy

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 18.3 months vs 3.6 months placebo (HR 0.21, p<0.001); ORR 64.8%; median OS 52.2 months in responders; however primarily a second/third-line option for this patient's early-stage disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for RAI-refractory DTC; widely available globally; established prescribing infrastructure at oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity profile: hypertension (68%), diarrhea (59%), fatigue (59%), decreased appetite (50%), weight loss (46%); dose reductions needed in 68% of patients; serious AEs in 51%

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor effective regardless of BRAF status; not biomarker-specific but applicable to all RAI-refractory DTC; less precise than BRAF-targeted therapy for this BRAF V600E+ patient

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib is a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR1-3, FGFR1-4, PDGFRalpha, KIT, and RET. It inhibits tumor angiogenesis and directly suppresses tumor cell proliferation through blockade of multiple oncogenic signaling pathways. FDA approved for progressive, RAI-refractory DTC.

key_evidence.study_name: SELECT Trial (Study of E7080 Lenvatinib in Differentiated Cancer of the Thyroid)

biomarker_requirements: RAI-refractory or RAI-ineligible differentiated thyroid cancer

treatments.name: Sorafenib (Nexavar)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 DECISION trial showed significant PFS improvement; first FDA-approved systemic therapy for RAI-refractory DTC (2013); well-established evidence base

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 10.8 months vs 5.8 months placebo (HR 0.59, p<0.001); lower ORR (12.2%) than lenvatinib; no significant OS benefit demonstrated due to crossover; typically second choice to lenvatinib

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for RAI-refractory DTC since 2013; widely available; generic formulations increasingly available reducing cost

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: hand-foot skin reaction (76%), diarrhea (69%), alopecia (67%), rash (50%); dose reductions in 64%; treatment discontinuation in 19%

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor with activity against BRAF, VEGFR, PDGFR, RET, and KIT; has some BRAF inhibitory activity but not selective; effective in RAI-refractory DTC regardless of mutation status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Sorafenib is a multi-kinase inhibitor that targets RAF (including wild-type BRAF), VEGFR1-3, PDGFR-beta, KIT, FLT3, and RET. It inhibits tumor angiogenesis and cell proliferation through blockade of the MAPK/ERK pathway and receptor tyrosine kinases.

treatments.name: Encorafenib + Binimetinib (BRAF/MEK Inhibition - Alternative)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 Japanese trial showed 47.1% ORR in DTC and 80% in ATC; approved in Japan for BRAF V600E thyroid cancer (May 2024); not FDA-approved for thyroid indication

survival_benefit.rationale: 12-month PFS rate 78.8%, OS rate 81.8%; ongoing response rate 90.9% at 12 months; median PFS/OS not reached; potentially favorable efficacy vs dabrafenib/trametinib

accessibility.rationale: Approved in Japan for BRAF V600E thyroid cancer; not FDA-approved for this indication in USA; encorafenib approved for other BRAF V600E cancers (melanoma, CRC) so off-label use possible

safety_profile.rationale: Favorable safety: grade 3 AEs in only 27.3%; no grade 4-5 events; most common grade 3 AE was lipase increase (18.2%); potentially better tolerated than dabrafenib/trametinib

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets BRAF V600E mutation; dual BRAF+MEK inhibition as alternative to dabrafenib+trametinib; molecularly matched to patient's BRAF V600E alteration

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Encorafenib selectively inhibits BRAF V600E kinase with a longer target residence time than dabrafenib, providing more sustained MAPK pathway suppression. Binimetinib inhibits MEK1/2. The combination provides dual-pathway blockade with potentially improved tolerability compared to dabrafenib/trametinib.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase 2 Encorafenib + Binimetinib in BRAF V600E Thyroid Cancer (Japan)

treatments.availability: Approved in Japan (May 2024); Off-label in USA

treatments.name: Redifferentiation Therapy (BRAF/MEK Inhibitor + RAI)

evidence_level.rationale: Multiple Phase 2 trials (ERRITI and others) with consistent positive results; International Thyroid Oncology Group consensus statement published 2025; growing evidence base but no Phase 3 trial yet

survival_benefit.rationale: Iodine uptake restoration in 33-95% of RAI-refractory patients; tumor response rates 11-80% after restored RAI therapy; potential for durable complete responses by combining targeted therapy with RAI

accessibility.rationale: Available at specialized academic centers with nuclear medicine and molecular oncology expertise; requires BRAF mutation testing and serial imaging; not yet standard of care

safety_profile.rationale: Short-term BRAF/MEK inhibitor exposure (2-6 weeks) minimizes cumulative toxicity; RAI toxicity profile well-established; combining modalities does not significantly increase adverse events

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly relevant for BRAF V600E+ PTC; BRAF/MEK inhibition restores NIS expression specifically in BRAF-mutant tumors; molecularly rational approach for this patient's mutation

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Short-term BRAF/MEK inhibition (dabrafenib/trametinib or selumetinib for 2-6 weeks) reverses MAPK-driven dedifferentiation of thyroid cancer cells, restoring sodium-iodide symporter (NIS) expression and iodine avidity. This enables subsequent RAI therapy to be effective in previously RAI-refractory lesions. Preclinical data suggests maximal redifferentiation occurs within 8-12 days of MAPK inhibition.

key_evidence.journal: Clinical Cancer Research / Lancet Diabetes Endocrinology

biomarker_requirements: RAI-refractory disease confirmed on diagnostic scan

treatments.name: Cabozantinib (Cabometyx)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 COSMIC-311 trial demonstrated 80% reduction in progression risk; FDA approved 2021 for RAI-refractory DTC after prior VEGFR-targeted therapy

survival_benefit.rationale: Significant PFS improvement (HR 0.22, p<0.001) vs placebo in COSMIC-311; positioned as second-line after lenvatinib/sorafenib failure; important option but primarily for advanced refractory disease

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for RAI-refractory DTC after prior VEGFR therapy; available at oncology centers; high cost ($20,000+/month)

safety_profile.rationale: Significant toxicity: palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia (46%), hypertension (28%), fatigue (27%), diarrhea (25%); dose reductions common; hemorrhage risk

biomarker_match.rationale: Multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR, MET, AXL, RET; not BRAF-specific but has broad anti-tumor activity in DTC; relevant as later-line option

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cabozantinib is a multi-kinase inhibitor targeting VEGFR2, MET, AXL, RET, KIT, and FLT3. It inhibits angiogenesis via VEGFR blockade and overcomes resistance mechanisms through concurrent MET and AXL inhibition. FDA approved as second-line for RAI-refractory DTC after prior VEGFR-targeted therapy.
biomarker_requirements: RAI-refractory DTC with prior VEGFR-targeted therapy
treatments.availability: FDA Approved for RAI-refractory DTC (second-line after VEGFR therapy)

treatments.name: External Beam Radiation Therapy (EBRT)

evidence_level.rationale: American Head and Neck Society consensus statement; retrospective data showing improved locoregional control; no Phase 3 RCT but strong observational evidence for specific indications (gross residual, pT4)

survival_benefit.rationale: Significantly improved locoregional control (95.2% vs 67.5% at 5 years) in locally advanced PTC; no OS improvement demonstrated; primarily for unresectable or gross residual disease, not typical for this patient's T3N1a stage

accessibility.rationale: Widely available at radiation oncology centers; standard IMRT/VMAT techniques used; well-established treatment infrastructure

safety_profile.rationale: Acute toxicity: mucositis, dysphagia, skin erythema, xerostomia; late effects: fibrosis, carotid stenosis, secondary malignancy risk; generally reserved for cases where benefit outweighs risk

biomarker_match.rationale: Not biomarker-dependent; considered for locally advanced or unresectable disease regardless of molecular profile; BRAF V600E may indicate more aggressive local disease warranting consideration

treatments.mechanism_of_action: External beam radiation therapy delivers targeted high-energy photons to the thyroid bed and cervical lymph node regions, causing DNA damage and cell death in residual cancer cells. Recommended doses are 66-70 Gy for gross disease, ~60 Gy for high-risk microscopic disease, and ~54 Gy for low-risk areas. Modern techniques (IMRT/VMAT) minimize dose to surrounding structures.

key_evidence.study_name: AHNS Statement on EBRT for DTC Locoregional Control

treatments.name: Vemurafenib (Zelboraf) Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 data in BRAF V600E thyroid cancer showing modest ORR (26-38%); no Phase 3 trial in thyroid cancer; approved for BRAF V600E melanoma but off-label for thyroid

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 26-38% as monotherapy in RAI-refractory PTC, lower than combination BRAF+MEK inhibition; weaker antitumor activity than dabrafenib+trametinib; mainly historical significance

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for BRAF V600E melanoma; used off-label for thyroid cancer; available at oncology centers but combination BRAF/MEK preferred

safety_profile.rationale: Photosensitivity (52%), rash, arthralgia, fatigue; risk of cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (20-30%); paradoxical MAPK activation without MEK inhibitor co-administration

biomarker_match.rationale: Directly targets BRAF V600E; however monotherapy inferior to combination with MEK inhibitor due to adaptive resistance

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Vemurafenib selectively inhibits BRAF V600E kinase, blocking constitutive MAPK pathway activation. As monotherapy, it provides initial tumor control but is limited by paradoxical MAPK pathway activation and adaptive resistance. Generally inferior to BRAF+MEK inhibitor combinations.

key_evidence.study_name: Vemurafenib in BRAF V600E Thyroid Cancer Phase 2

treatments.availability: FDA Approved for melanoma; Off-label for thyroid cancer

treatments.name: Lenvatinib + Pembrolizumab Combination

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 ATLEP trial showed 34.3% ORR in ATC/PDTC combined; 75% ORR in poorly differentiated thyroid cancer; emerging data for differentiated thyroid cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS 10 months in ATC; median OS 11 months in ATC; 75% ORR in PDTC is very promising; limited specific data in PTC but rationale strong for RAI-refractory cases

accessibility.rationale: Both drugs FDA approved individually; combination used off-label or in clinical trials; available at academic centers; high combined cost

safety_profile.rationale: Additive toxicities from both agents: hypertension, immune-related AEs (thyroiditis, hepatitis, colitis), fatigue, diarrhea; requires close monitoring for immune-mediated events

biomarker_match.rationale: Not BRAF-specific; BRAF V600E may upregulate PD-L1, potentially enhancing pembrolizumab response; lenvatinib targets VEGFR regardless of BRAF status; immunogenically cold tumors may limit efficacy

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lenvatinib provides anti-angiogenic and direct anti-tumor activity through multi-kinase inhibition (VEGFR, FGFR, PDGFR, RET, KIT). Pembrolizumab blocks PD-1, restoring T-cell anti-tumor immunity. Lenvatinib may enhance immunotherapy by modulating the tumor microenvironment, reducing immunosuppressive VEGF signaling and increasing T-cell infiltration.
biomarker_requirements: PD-L1 expression may predict response (not required)

treatments.name: Pembrolizumab (Keytruda) Monotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: KEYNOTE-158 Phase 2 data in DTC: 6.8% ORR among 103 patients; FDA approved for MSI-H/TMB-H solid tumors (tissue-agnostic); limited single-agent efficacy in thyroid cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: Only 6.8% ORR in previously treated PTC/FTC; median duration of response 18.4 months suggests durable benefit in responders; limited overall impact as monotherapy in DTC

accessibility.rationale: FDA approved for MSI-H/TMB-H solid tumors and multiple other cancers; widely available; applicable if PTC has MSI-H or TMB-H phenotype

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized immune-related AE profile; thyroid dysfunction, rash, fatigue, colitis, hepatitis; most manageable with steroids; lower toxicity than multi-TKIs

biomarker_match.rationale: BRAF V600E may upregulate PD-L1 expression; however most PTCs are immunologically cold; limited efficacy without combination therapy; MSI-H/TMB-H testing recommended

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody blocking PD-1 receptor on T cells, restoring anti-tumor immune response. In thyroid cancer, BRAF V600E-driven tumors may have higher PD-L1 expression, providing rationale for checkpoint blockade. However, PTC is generally immunologically cold with low tumor mutational burden.
biomarker_requirements: MSI-H or TMB-H for tissue-agnostic indication

clinical_trials.title: Encorafenib + Binimetinib in BRAF V600E-Mutated Metastatic Thyroid Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 54.5% overall (47.1% DTC, 80% ATC); 12-month PFS 78.8%, OS 81.8%; no grade 4-5 AEs

clinical_trials.title: Enhancing Radioiodine Incorporation into RAI-Refractory Thyroid Cancer with MAPK Inhibition
clinical_trials.early_results: Genotype-guided MAPK modulation restored iodine avidity; trametinib for BRAF-WT, dabrafenib/trametinib for BRAF-MUT; iodine uptake restoration 33-95%

clinical_trials.title: Phase II ATLEP Trial: Lenvatinib/Pembrolizumab in Metastasized ATC and PDTC
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 34.3% overall; ATC: 51.9% PR, median PFS 10 months, median OS 11 months; PDTC: 75% PR

clinical_trials.title: Pembrolizumab Monotherapy in Previously Treated Advanced Thyroid Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: ORR 6.8% in DTC (7/103 patients); median DOR 18.4 months in responders; limited monotherapy efficacy

clinical_trials.title: Cabozantinib vs Placebo in RAI-Refractory DTC After Prior VEGFR Therapy
clinical_trials.early_results: PFS HR 0.22 (p<0.001); 80% reduction in progression risk vs placebo; ORR 15%; established cabozantinib as second-line standard

clinical_trials.title: Selpercatinib vs Standard of Care in RET-Mutant Medullary Thyroid Cancer
clinical_trials.early_results: Selpercatinib superior to cabozantinib/vandetanib as frontline MTC therapy; FDA full approval June 2024; not applicable to this BRAF+ patient but demonstrates precision oncology paradigm

clinical_trials.title: Dabrafenib + Trametinib in BRAF V600E-Mutant Rare Cancers (Basket Trial)
clinical_trials.early_results: ATC cohort: ORR 56%, 3 CRs; median OS 15 months (historic <6 months for ATC); led to FDA approval for ATC; basis for tissue-agnostic approval

combination_strategies.rationale: Short-term BRAF/MEK inhibition restores NIS expression and iodine avidity in BRAF V600E-mutant RAI-refractory tumors (redifferentiation). Sequential administration: 2-6 weeks of dabrafenib/trametinib followed by RAI therapy. Iodine uptake restoration in 33-95% of patients, enabling effective RAI treatment in previously refractory disease.

Lenvatinib's anti-VEGFR activity modulates tumor microenvironment, reducing immunosuppression and enhancing T-cell infiltration. Pembrolizumab restores anti-tumor immunity via PD-1 blockade. ATLEP trial demonstrated 34.3% ORR in advanced thyroid cancer. Synergistic mechanism: angiogenic inhibition plus immune checkpoint release.

BRAF/MEK inhibition increases tumor antigen expression, MHC class I upregulation, and T-cell infiltration. Adding PD-1 blockade may prolong responses beyond targeted therapy alone. Retrospective data show 42% PR when pembrolizumab added at progression on kinase inhibitors. Addresses adaptive immune resistance to targeted therapy.

combination_strategies.evidence_level: Standard of Care / Level I evidence
combination_strategies.rationale: Sequential multimodal approach: surgery removes macroscopic disease, RAI ablates microscopic residual, and TSH suppression removes growth stimulus for remaining TSH-responsive cells. This is the established standard combination for intermediate-risk PTC with 10-year OS >97%. TSH targets per 2025 ATA guidelines based on response assessment.

Sequential multi-TKI strategy for RAI-refractory DTC: first-line lenvatinib or sorafenib, followed by cabozantinib at progression. COSMIC-311 established cabozantinib as second-line after VEGFR therapy failure with 80% reduction in progression risk. MET/AXL inhibition by cabozantinib may overcome resistance mechanisms to first-line VEGFR-targeted therapy.

supportive_care.evidence: ATA Statement on Postoperative Hypoparathyroidism (Thyroid, 2018); Meta-analyses of post-thyroidectomy calcium supplementation
supportive_care.benefit: Prevents symptomatic hypocalcemia after total thyroidectomy; recommended minimum 3 g/day oral calcium for 2 weeks post-surgery with calcitriol 1 mcg/day for high-risk patients. Transient hypoparathyroidism occurs in up to 50% after total thyroidectomy; permanent in 1-3%.

supportive_care.evidence: Post-thyroidectomy voice and swallowing rehabilitation guidelines; prospective studies showing benefit of early speech therapy
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses voice changes (transient dysphonia 12-20%), vocal cord paresis/paralysis, and swallowing difficulties after thyroidectomy. Speech therapy exercises strengthen vocal cords and improve swallowing mechanics. Early intervention improves outcomes and quality of life.

supportive_care.approach: Physical Rehabilitation and Neck Mobility Program
supportive_care.evidence: Post-thyroidectomy rehabilitation studies; physical therapy for neck stiffness and shoulder dysfunction after neck dissection
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses neck stiffness, shoulder pain, and limited range of motion post-surgery, particularly after central neck dissection. Targeted exercises restore strength and flexibility. Spinal accessory nerve preservation techniques minimize shoulder dysfunction.

supportive_care.approach: Psychosocial Support and Cancer Survivorship Care
supportive_care.evidence: NCCN Survivorship Guidelines; ACS cancer survivorship care planning recommendations
supportive_care.benefit: Addresses anxiety about recurrence, body image concerns, hypothyroidism-related fatigue and mood changes, and long-term quality of life. PTC has excellent prognosis but requires lifelong surveillance which can cause chronic anxiety. Structured survivorship programs improve adherence to follow-up and quality of life.

supportive_care.evidence: ATA 2025 Management Guidelines; serial thyroglobulin monitoring as tumor marker for DTC recurrence
supportive_care.benefit: Post-thyroidectomy thyroglobulin (Tg) serves as highly sensitive tumor marker for recurrence detection. Serial Tg with anti-Tg antibodies at 6-12 month intervals; rising Tg triggers imaging workup. Enables early detection and treatment of recurrence when most manageable. Stimulated Tg testing for risk stratification.
